data.title: CARsgen Therapeutics expands CAR-T manufacturing in Shanghai with new agreements. Stock Code: 2171.HK.
data.description: In Shanghai on February 12, 2026, CARsgen Therapeutics Holdings Limited (Stock Code: 2171.HK), a firm specializing in cutting-edge CAR T-cell therapies, disclosed a key development. The company, via its subsidiary CARsgen Diagnostics, has entered a Strategic Cooperation agreement.

data.title: Suspected human trafficking funds in crypto surged by 85% in 2025, hitting millions; a CSAM site received over $530,000.
data.description: According to a report by Chainalysis, it was revealed that cryptocurrency transactions related to trafficking activities have surged to amounts totaling hundreds of millions of dollars. The report highlighted a particularly alarming case where a site involved in distributing Child Sexual Abuse Material (CSAM) received over $530,000 in cryptocurrency transactions. This data underscores the growing concern regarding the impact of cryptocurrencies on illicit activities and the urgent need for increased scrutiny and regulation in this sector.

data.title: ArtCreativity acquires Wooly Willy® brand from PlayMonster Group, marking a new era for the iconic toy.
data.description: ArtCreativity, a company based in New Hampton, New York, revealed on February 12, 2026, that they have successfully acquired the renowned Wooly Willy® brand from PlayMonster Group. This acquisition marks a significant milestone in the toy industry, as it ushers in a new phase of evolution and worldwide growth for one of the most enduring novelty toys. Wooly Willy® has been a beloved classic since its initial introduction and has enjoyed a longstanding presence in the market. This acquisition by ArtCreativity signals a fresh chapter for the brand's development and signals its global expansion.

data.title: Innovent starts Phase 3 trial of IBI354 for HER2-positive breast cancer, Feb. 12, 2026. Details at PRNewswire.
data.description: In a recent press release dated February 12, 2026, it was announced that Innovent Biologics, Inc., a leading biopharmaceutical company based in both SAN FRANCISCO and SUZHOU, China, is focused on the development, manufacturing, and commercialization of top-notch medicines targeting oncology, cardiovascular, and metabolic conditions. The company, listed as HKEX: 01801, is dedicated to producing high-quality pharmaceuticals aimed at improving patient outcomes in these critical areas of healthcare.

data.title: MPT declares $0.09 quarterly dividend, annual meeting on May 28, 2026 in Birmingham, record date March 12.
data.description: The company MPT (NYSE: MPT) has declared a regular quarterly cash dividend of $0.09 per share of common stock. The dividend will be paid on April 9, 2026, to stockholders of record on March 12, 2026. Additionally, MPT announced that its annual meeting of stockholders will take place on May 28, 2026, at 10:30 a.m. Central Time at the UAB Collat School of Business in Birmingham, Alabama.

data.title: Stingray to trade shares on TSX under one ticker starting Feb. 13, 2026, for subordinate and variable subordinate voting shares.
data.description: In Montreal on February 10, 2026, Stingray Group Inc., a prominent connected streaming media company globally, revealed that as of February 13, 2026, its subordinate voting shares and variable subordinate voting shares will be consolidated under a single ticker on the Toronto Stock Exchange. This move aims to streamline trading activities for investors.

data.title: Americans are split on 2026 economy; 50% expect stock market gains & 49% forecast economic growth. 62% predict inflation climb, 50% foresee higher joblessness. Optimism rebounded from tariff-driven pessimism in 2025; now 25% predict equities decline vs 61% in early 2025. Optimism on economic growth increased from 38% to 49%. Economic sentiment survey conducted by Gallup Jan 2-17.
data.description: A recent Gallup poll conducted from Jan. 2–17, 2026, shows that Americans are cautiously optimistic about the economy and stock market, with 50% anticipating stock market gains and 49% predicting economic growth. However, concerns remain about rising inflation (62%) and unemployment (50%). This sentiment indicates a divided nation on the future of the economy. The survey results indicate a recovery from April 2025 when public confidence plummeted due to tariffs imposed by President Donald Trump. During that time, only 29% expected stock market gains, a significant drop from 61% in early 2025. Currently, stock market optimism has rebounded by 21 percentage points, with only 25% expecting equities to decline in the next six months. Economic growth optimism has also risen from 38% in April to 49% currently. The number of Americans predicting a shrinking economy has decreased to 36%. This turnaround in sentiment suggests a shift towards positive economic outlook, despite lingering concerns about inflation and unemployment.

data.title: January jobs report with significant revisions to historical data lands Wednesday at 8:30 a.m. ET. Forecast: payrolls up 45,000, unemployment rate stay at 4.4%. Market eagerly awaits 'Super Bowl of jobs reports'.
data.description: The upcoming January jobs report in the U.S. is highly anticipated due to its annual benchmark revisions tied to the Quarterly Census of Employment and Wages, updates to the birth-death model, and new seasonal adjustment factors. The report is described as "the Super Bowl of jobs reports" by Bank of America economist Shruti Mishra. This report is crucial for markets and Federal Reserve policy, as it could potentially change the historical payroll path, impacting the debate on whether the labor market is smoothly cooling or abruptly losing momentum. Bank of America forecasts January nonfarm payrolls to rise by 45,000, below the consensus, with an expected unemployment rate of 4.4%. The softer payroll forecast is due to expected downward revisions linked to the updated birth-death model. This data release will include key numbers like the employment report, unemployment rate, and revisions to prior data that could reshape the narrative about labor momentum into 2026.

data.title: Dow closes at record high, Nasdaq's winning streak ends in mixed market action Tuesday.
data.description: Today, the stock market experienced mixed results with the major indexes closing after a volatile day of trading. The Dow Jones Industrial Average achieved a new record high, while the Nasdaq ended its winning streak. The fluctuations in the market reflected uncertainty among investors. The Dow closed at an all-time high, outperforming other indexes. Overall, the market showed a diverse performance on Tuesday.
